title: 2026 Reno Police Department Beat-Based Town Halls and Neighborhood Advisory Board Presentations
resolved_text:
Memorandum
DATE: January 26, 2026
TO: Mayor and City Council
THROUGH: Jackie Bryant, City Manager
FROM: Kathryn Nance, Police Chief
Jenifer Alvarez, Council Relations Liaison
DEPT: Reno Police Department
City Manager's Office
SUBJECT: 2026 Reno Police Department Beat-Based Town Halls and Neighborhood Advisory Board Presentations

In 2026, the Reno Police Department (RPD) will expand the Reno Police Department's community engagement efforts through a series of police beat-based town halls and annual presentations to Neighborhood Advisory Boards (NABs). These structured forums will provide residents, City leadership, and RPD staff with an open and transparent setting to share timely public safety information, hear localized concerns, and partner on practical, community-driven solutions.

Each town hall will emphasize:
• Beat-specific crime trends and statistics
• Beat-specific public safety concerns
• Community policing initiatives
• Mental health and policing

Five town halls are planned and will be organized by police beat to ensure comprehensive geographic coverage: two in the North Beat (Wards 1, 4, and 5), one in the Central Beat (Wards 1, 2, and 3), and two in the South Beat (Wards 2, 3, and 6). Each town hall will feature a moderated presentation followed by a facilitated question-and-answer session. The inaugural town hall will be held on February 12th, 2026 at North Valleys High School, which can accommodate up to 500 attendees and supports live streaming by City staff. Doors will open at 5:00 p.m., and the program will begin at 6:00 p.m.

To support consistent engagement and accessibility, informational resource booths will be available before and after the program. The City of Reno's Rolling Recreation Center will also be onsite, offering yard games, sports balls, table games, and arts and crafts at no cost to attendees, and food trucks will be stationed in the parking area with items available for purchase. This model will be replicated for future town halls to ensure consistent services and outreach. A detailed agenda is being finalized to support effective moderation, time management, and discussion of community priorities. In advance of the first town hall, community questions and feedback will be collected via askrpd@reno.gov to help prioritize topics and ensure each session is responsive to the most pressing beat-specific concerns.

The Communications Department is coordinating promotion of the town halls as details are finalized. A press release is in development, and a media availability with Chief Nance and Mayor Schieve is scheduled on January 29, from 1:00 to 1:30 p.m., at the Public Safety Center to launch this year's events. Outreach will also include newsletters, social media, and broadcast placements in advance of each town hall, following a consistent promotional cadence.

Future anticipated 2026 dates are as follows (beat assignments confirmed). Specific venues and dates will be published once finalized:
• Late April 2026 (South Beat)
• Late June 2026 (Central Beat)
• Early August 2026 (North Beat)
• Late October 2026 (South Beat)

Finalizing dates and locations remains a priority to ensure timely public notice and adequate opportunity for community participation.

In addition to the town halls, RPD will conduct annual presentations at all Neighborhood Advisory Boards (NABs). These presentations will focus on ward-specific public safety concerns, crime statistics, and RPD initiatives supporting community policing.

Council will receive invitations to all town halls and advance notice of Council's scheduled NAB presentation dates once the calendar is finalized. For each town hall, Council will have a designated greeting area to engage with attendees before and after the program. Additional information will be provided as details are confirmed.

fields.authors: Kathryn Nance, Police Chief; Jenifer Alvarez, Council Relations Liaison
fields.tldr: The Reno Police Department will host five beat-based town halls across the city in 2026, organized by North, Central, and South police beats, to share public safety information and gather community input. The inaugural event is scheduled for February 12, 2026, at North Valleys High School, with doors opening at 5:00 p.m. and the program beginning at 6:00 p.m. RPD will also conduct annual presentations at all Neighborhood Advisory Boards focused on ward-specific crime statistics and community policing initiatives. A media availability with Chief Nance and Mayor Schieve is set for January 29 to launch the series.
fields.key_stats: Five town halls planned: two in the North Beat (Wards 1, 4, 5), one in the Central Beat (Wards 1, 2, 3), two in the South Beat (Wards 2, 3, 6); inaugural venue (North Valleys High School) accommodates up to 500 attendees and supports live streaming.
fields.key_decisions: Expansion of RPD community engagement through beat-based town halls and annual NAB presentations; adoption of a replicable event model including resource booths, Rolling Recreation Center activities at no cost, and food trucks for each town hall.
fields.action_items: Finalize detailed agenda for February 12 town hall; collect community questions via askrpd@reno.gov ahead of first event; issue press release and conduct media availability on January 29 (1:00–1:30 p.m.) at the Public Safety Center; finalize and publish specific venues and dates for remaining four town halls; send Council invitations and advance NAB presentation schedule once calendar is confirmed.

dates: January 29, 2026, 1:00–1:30 p.m. (media availability at Public Safety Center)
February 12, 2026 (inaugural town hall, North Valleys High School; doors 5:00 p.m., program 6:00 p.m.)
